macro_summary: The Nasdaq rallied 1.5% on Thursday as the Iran peace deal took effect and tankers resumed transit through Hormuz, driving WTI up 1.5% to $75.69 but still down 14% for the week. The FOMC held rates steady, raised its 2026 inflation forecast to 3.6%, and penciled a potential rate hike — a hawkish surprise that left the 10-year yield little changed at 4.45%. AI chips reignited: SOXX surged 4.5%, led by Intel (+7.8%) and Micron (+5.2%) on Apple foundry news and memory strength.
commodities_note: WTI bounced 1.5% to $75.69 as Hormuz tanker traffic resumed, but the weekly decline of 14% reflects the market’s aggressive pricing of returning supply. Gold dropped 3.3% to $4,196 on peace-driven risk-on, while the dollar index rose 0.6% on hawkish FOMC guidance. VIX collapsed 11% to 16.4, the lowest since the conflict began.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to BUY — 50-day SMA reclaimed, TPU merchant push re-rating.
writeup.what_to_do: Google has recaptured its 50-day average and is reportedly building a rival AI chip business (WSJ, Jun 19), leveraging its custom TPU silicon to compete with NVDA’s merchant model. This is a structural catalyst that could re-rate the stock as an AI infrastructure play, not just an ad-driven cloud. The headline R:R is 12.5:1 distorted by a 0.8% stop; sizing R:R to structural support at $343.63 is 1.5:1 — adequate for a starter. Entry zone $365–370, invalidation at $364.44 (50-day). Starter position — fragility: the merchant TPU strategy requires validated hyperscaler wins beyond Anthropic. BUY.

writeup.headline: Bloom +13.3% — consolidating RCP after Project Jupiter gap.
writeup.what_to_do: The Oracle Project Jupiter gap on June 3 triggered the regime-change process; BE is in session 11 of the 25-session consolidation gate. The catalyst is a signed contract with a named customer (Oracle), and the price needs to prove it can hold above $302.85 without breaking below the post-gap SMA50. Graduation triggers: Path A — retest of the new post-gap SMA50 (currently ~$261) on a pullback; Path B — breakout above the 8%-band high ($310.30 * 1.02 = $316.50) with volume. Failure triggers: 5+ closes below $287.32 * 0.95 = $272.95 AND below post-gap SMA50, or 3+ closes below the pre-gap close of $302.85. No entry until graduation.

The 50-day retest at €50.36 is 16% below — a pullback there would be a reasonable entry for a long-term compounder. No action until that reset.

thesis_highlights: WeRide Q1 2026 results (reported May 13 2026): revenue +58% YoY on Robotaxi fleet expansion and L2++ ADAS commercialisation — cite the print specifics when news references it.
WeRide bull case rests on B2B fleet licensing (Uber, Grab, Bosch) + multi-country Robotaxi deployment — evaluate on commercial traction, not autonomy benchmarks vs Waymo/Tesla.
ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

writeup.headline: WeRide Zurich robotaxi expansion adds second European leg — accumulate.
writeup.what_to_do: The planned Zurich robotaxi service with Uber (per Just Auto, Jun 17) is the second European deployment after Madrid, adding a concrete revenue milestone to the B2B fleet licensing thesis. R:R 2.0:1 to nearest resistance at $6.98, invalidation at $6.03 (support), no earnings for 41 days, VIX 16.4 — all mechanical gates pass. Entry zone $6.30-6.40, stop below $6.03. Wide-stop R:R not applicable (no structural support). Half position — ADR/delisting risk remains the standing thesis invalidator. ACCUMULATE — starter position. [paper]
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if WRD fails to reclaim SMA50 ($7.38) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain hard-blocked on extension — the best price action is in OCBC, which continues to outperform on profit growth but is still 8.3% above its 50-day. DBS leads the extension at 9.2%, and UOB’s declining revenue undercuts its seemingly cheap valuation. The dual NIM headwind from US10Y above 4.20% and MAS S$NEER appreciation is unrelenting; the next potential relief valve is October’s MAS decision.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression persists; no entry until rate catalyst or pullback to 50-day averages.
singapore.key_development: US10Y held at 4.45% after a hawkish FOMC, keeping the NIM pressure firmly in place for the cluster.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster surged 4.5% (SOXX +4.45%) led by Intel on the Apple rumor and SK Hynix on HBM4E sampling. But the rally is dangerously concentrated: 11 of 13 names are hard-blocked on extension, and the two that aren’t — NVDA and AVGO — are only just recapturing their 50-day averages. The memory names (MU +55%, 000660.KS +64%) are priced for perfection with earnings imminent; the downside is asymmetric.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — AI demand visibility is high, but the entry points are terrible except for NVDA/AVGO near the 50-day.
semiconductors.key_development: Intel’s unconfirmed Apple deal sparked a 7.8% surge; ASML faces fresh EUV leakage concerns from the US Commerce Secretary.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is the most divergent cluster: Google has reclaimed its 50-day and is building a merchant TPU narrative, while Microsoft languishes 8% below its average on OpenAI dependency fears, and Amazon is recovering but still below its 50-day. Palantir’s 62x forward PE keeps it out of reach. The cluster median PE of 25x provides a valuation anchor; MSFT at 19.6x is the value outlier, but the OpenAI overhang caps upside.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — Google’s TPU optionality and Amazon’s Trainium merchant push offer upside, but Microsoft’s concentration risk keeps the cluster from a unified bullish stance.
big_tech.key_development: WSJ reports Google is building a merchant AI chip business, directly challenging NVDA; Amazon also exploring external Trainium sales.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL pulls back 5-8% after S&P 500 inclusion on June 22, with optical revenue growth >30% YoY in next quarter.

interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for breakout above $145 on volume after NVDA photonics demand commentary; add if trading at or below its 50-day average.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung’s HBM3E qualification news surfaces with a specific NVDA design win, trading below ₩80,000.

active_risks: Hormuz reopening gains momentum as tankers resume transit under US-Iran interim peace deal; traffic to be restored to full capacity within 30 days (per Reuters, Jun 18).
US Commerce Secretary Lutnick raises concern with ASML that an EUV machine may have reached China, violating export controls (per Bloomberg/Reuters, Jun 19).
Fed Chair Warsh holds rates, raises 2026 inflation forecast to 3.6%, signals potential rate hike — hawkish pivot amid easing energy costs (per CNBC, Jun 18).
US-China technology tensions persist with the Pentagon blacklist, but no new names added; Canada caps Chinese EV imports under US pressure (per Newsweek, Jun 17).
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz deal’s implementation is steadily pulling oil lower, unleashing a risk-on rotation that has lifted SOXX 4.5% and compressed VIX to 16.4. However, the hawkish FOMC puts a ceiling on rate-sensitive tech valuations, keeping semis extended but volatile. ASML faces a fresh geopolitical overhang from the EUV leakage report, while Singapore banks suffer from higher-for-longer USD rates. The peace dividend is visible in lower commodity prices but is being offset by central bank hawkishness.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: The Hormuz reopening accelerated with four supertankers exiting the strait; US Commerce Secretary directly raised EUV leakage concerns with ASML executives.

base.description: Hormuz traffic normalizes within 30 days, WTI stabilizes around $75, and the Fed holds off on a rate hike. Semis remain bid but consolidation corrects the extreme extensions, allowing selective entry in NVDA/AVGO near the 50-day. Singapore banks drift sideways with no NIM catalyst.

optimistic.description: Full Hormuz reopening plus a US-China trade détente (Trump-Xi meeting in September) removes oil risk premium and export control overhangs. WTI drops to $65, US10Y falls below 4.0%, igniting a broad AI capex rally. NVDA, AMZN, and ASML surge; Singapore banks re-rate as NIM pressure eases on lower rates.

pessimistic.description: Iran backtracks on the deal, Hormuz traffic stalls, and WTI spikes back above $95. The Fed is forced to hike, crushing tech multiples and sending semis into correction. SK Hynix and MU crash on demand fears; Singapore banks plunge as credit costs rise; safe-haven gold rallies.

wildcard.description: The EUV leakage report escalates into a full-blown US-Dutch chip sanctions crisis, restricting ASML’s ability to sell to non-China customers as well due to tech-transfer fears. Semis supply chain dislocates; AMD and INTC benefit as alternatives, but the overall sector takes a sharp hit.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain cash-heavy posture; the peace rally is real but narrow — only NVDA and GOOG offer actionable entries, and both are starter-sized. Avoid energy and extended memory names.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautious — wide extension across semis bars new entries despite a favorable macro backdrop.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 16 of 28 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — the peace rally has pushed semis to extreme levels, leaving only a few near-term entry opportunities (NVDA, GOOG, WRD). The Hormuz deal and hawkish Fed create a fragile equilibrium: any oil spike or rate surprise could trigger a swift correction in the overextended names.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop.
